input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: You can use `dotnet watch test` to automatically re-run tests when files change. Let me start it for you:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. It will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file change.

Currently there's a compilation error in `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` line 25 — `ArgumentException` can't be found (likely missing a `using System;` directive). The watcher is waiting for you to fix it and will rebuild automatically on save.
